runtime_target: static and data-backed chapter dossier system
operational_end_state: A reader or editor can inspect chapter argument, source state, revision status, and public-facing presentation logic.
core_modules: chapter registry | argument backbone | visual/title gate | handoff
source_strategy: Use sources to govern chapter argument, voice register, public title, source burden, and cross-chapter handoff. Each source should either thicken the chapter claim, sharpen a counterclaim, or constrain the public formulation.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.
source_gap: passage-level close reading still required before quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | chapter uniqueness test: title, visual, source, and argument are chapter-specific
current_build_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
proof_to_full_build_gap: Build the first runnable or inspectable demo rather than leaving the project at protocol level.
first_full_build: Add chapter-level dossiers showing title logic, argument skeleton, source role, rewrite decision, and visual specificity for representative chapters.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
